Andy Stone Get in touch

Strategic Pitch | Tanium

From digital brochure to demand engine.

Tanium has great product-market fit — proven by industry reports and customer case studies. What's important now is building a modern digital demand strategy that can grow the company toward IPO.

Role

Sr. Dir. Demand Generation

Focus

First Touch to SQL

Image placeholder

Screenshot of current Tanium.com homepage

Introduction

I've spent nearly two decades helping companies grow through design programs, digital strategy initiatives, and marketing campaigns. When looking at any new company, I've always started with research to understand the baseline — so there's a shared understanding of where to grow from here.

Tanium needs a marketing leader who can help drive the go-to-market strategy to create awareness, drive qualified leads, and turn those leads into direct sales opportunities. Outside of one-off campaigns and responsive tactics, the most important thing is to have a repeatable demand engine that touches on all points of programs, channels, processes, and underlying technology to hit pipeline.

I'm coming at this from an outsider's perspective, so there's a chance that many of my recommendations are already in flight or there are internal reasons for going a different route. None of my suggestions are meant as a slight towards the teams — just sharing my perspective on a direction for demand generation for a digital-first company.

Download Resume →

The Plan

Modern Digital Demand

The role of Demand Generation encompasses every touchpoint of the customer journey. Starting at the top of the funnel is critical — more possibilities going into every test and idea down the road, and crucial in today's winner-takes-all environment.

The plan for the first year: work down the marketing funnel from first-awareness to sales handoff, fixing leaks and making improvements along the way.

Awareness

SEO · Paid · Social · GEO · 3rd Party Validation

Interest

A/B Testing · Next Steps · Asking Questions

Consideration

Demos · ROI Calculators · Chat · Simplified Forms

Intent

In-Person Demos · Webinars · Events · Engineering Showcase

Purchase

Working Hand in Hand with Sales

Technical Baseline

3.1s

Time to First Byte on key listing pages. The benchmark is under 500ms.

25

Blog Index Lighthouse performance score — the lowest on the site, with a Best Practices fail

7

Mandatory form fields required from cold traffic to request a demo

0

Active personalization paths delivered — despite 6Sense already being in the stack

"While the site has some issues with the performance scores, these are easily fixable with the correct focus and prioritization for the teams."

Image placeholder

Side-by-side: current Tanium.com nav (broken dropdown) vs. proposed fix

Top-level dropdown links are currently unreachable via click — they only trigger the menu. A basic usability failure on a flagship page.

Quick Hits

From auditing the site today, there are a variety of tactical changes that could be made immediately.

01

Traffic is declining

Per SimilarWeb, tanium.com gets ~267K visits/month — but traffic dropped 12.4%. This is likely due to the rise of GEO, with more research being done inside chat agents. Tanium needs to solidify its GEO/AIO strategy to account for the loss of traditional organic traffic.

02

Personalization is missing in action

A CISO at a Fortune 100 bank and an IT admin at a mid-market retailer currently see the exact same homepage. No persona-based content paths, no industry-specific landing pages, no ABM-personalized experiences. 6Sense is already in the stack — it could be used immediately.

03

Rotating hero carousels are conversion killers

The homepage hero includes a 3-slide auto-rotating carousel. This is one of the most well-documented conversion problems in B2B web design — the majority of visitors never see slides 2 or 3. The Gartner MQ win and brand narrative are largely invisible to most traffic.

04

The demo form is heavy for cold traffic

The "See a Demo" form requires 7 fields on first touch — company email, first/last name, job title, phone, company name, and country — with no lighter conversion path or progressive profiling option. This creates unnecessary friction at the most critical conversion moment.

05

Events calendar needs more owned events

Currently seeing 7 upcoming events listed — almost all third-party trade shows. The calendar needs more webinars and demo opportunities that prospective clients can try. Fewer form fields on these would help too.

06

Interactive conversion tools are absent

There aren't many places for prospects to explore on their own terms. ROI calculators, interactive product tours, and self-guided demos would help. Competitors like CrowdStrike are moving toward these to warm up leads earlier in the journey.

07

No visible nurture architecture

The site doesn't give visitors many next steps. When finishing an article or page, there should be "if you read this, here's what's next" content flows — tracked and used to fuel progressive profiling and further personalization.

08

JavaScript dependency breaks the site

The site fails entirely when JavaScript is disabled — a real problem for bot crawlers and accessibility. Delivering pages as static assets would fix this and meaningfully speed up the site for everyone.

Image placeholder

Current "See a Demo" form — 7 mandatory fields

Image placeholder

Concept: progressive profiling — lighter first touch

The current demo form requires 7 mandatory fields from cold traffic. Progressive profiling collects data over time — reducing friction without sacrificing lead quality.

A Test from My Time at NetApp

Asking Questions

One small but important test I ran with my team was to simply ask questions to our customers on the site. It started as a small test on one page, but grew into whole-site profiling efforts. The assumption: customers in the B2B space don't want to waste time, and will tell you what they're looking for if you make it easy. So we just asked: what do you want?

Tanium could benefit from a similar strategy — profiling the customer throughout the process and offering relevant information at every stage. Simply ask: what industry are you in? What are you looking to secure? What issues have you had in the past? It makes the site feel more human, and speeds up the entire sales process.

Image placeholder

Wireframe / concept: interactive intent-capture module

Why Me

I've built this exact kind of engine — and the results are on record.

Let's talk →

NetApp

Led a full transformation of NetApp's global demand engine — a 20,000-page site across 10 languages. In the first year after relaunch: doubled organic traffic, increased SQLs by 11%, and earned Forbes' #1 ranking in B2B digital experience. Delivered within budget, with the highest team satisfaction score in the organization.

Founded & Acquired

Founded Emerson Stone, a B2B design and strategy agency (clients: IBM, 3M, Wells Fargo, Twitter) — acquired by Sawaya Consulting. Before that, co-founded Mocavo, a genealogical search engine that grew to the third largest in the industry before acquisition by FindMyPast.

How I Work

I learn from sales and marketing teams first — to find out what's working, where investment is needed, and how to prioritize. I'm drawn to fast-moving environments where decisions are data-driven, and where I can be useful to a lot of teams, not just my own.

Perspectives on AI

Every single contributor in a marketing or sales organization should be using AI to speed up their workflows, create more efficiently, and discover new ways of approaching problems.

In all of these ways, I see AI as a supercharger for teams — never as a replacement.

This site was created quickly with the assistance of various AI tools because I thought it would be an interesting exercise for this application. I used Perplexity for public research, SEMRush for technical and GEO research, Claude Code to create the overall page, and Figma MCP to send assets directly into the layouts. The writing was done by hand because I needed to synthesize too many disparate sources — and the attempts at AI drafting were taking longer to edit than necessary for a one-off production like this.

Andy Stone

Prepared for Tanium, 2026
